title: dryopteris fibrillosa
intro: Dryopteris fibrillosa, also known as the fringewort, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of fringewort include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and even act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Fringewort can be prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in tinctures and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
